bibliographic.citation: A Noble Boke off Cookry ffor a Prynce Houssolde or Eny Other Estately Houssolde: Reprinted Verbatim from a Rare MS. in the Holkham Collection, ed. Mrs. Alexander Napier (London: Elliot Stock, 1882).
bibliographic.title: A Noble Boke off Cookry ffor a Prynce Houssolde or Eny Other Estately Houssolde

entities.label: Napier 1882 edition
notes: The main manuscript transcription occupies printed pages 1-123; Napier's introduction, appendix, glossary, and errata are separate modern editorial matter.
The title-page claim 'reprinted verbatim' is an edition statement, not independent proof that every spelling, punctuation mark, expansion, or visual feature exactly reproduces the manuscript.

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1882, and U-M independently marks the CME item derived from it No Copyright-United States.
rights.component: The 1882 edition, transcription, introduction, notes, appendix, and glossary

access.accessMethod: Exact shelfmark and title searches across Holkham, the Fitzwilliam label, Cambridge Digital Library, Scriptorium, Biblissima, Bridgeman Images, Digital Bodleian, and the Bodleian/MARCO Holkham microfilm collection
notes: No public whole-codex facsimile or selected-leaf manuscript images were found. This does not establish that MS 674 has never been digitized or photographed privately.
Bodleian shelfmark MS. Film 674 reproduces a group of other Holkham manuscripts and must not be confused with Holkham Hall MS 674.

access.accessMethod: Email Holkham's Library and Manuscripts team to request a research appointment and confirm access conditions for MS 674
access.format: Onsite consultation by prior arrangement, subject to access and preservation conditions
notes: The currently linked PDF includes archive study fees, but the applicability of those fees to this manuscript-library item should be confirmed directly.
access.provider: Holkham Hall, the Earl of Leicester and the Trustees of the Holkham Estate

access.accessMethod: Email the Library and Manuscripts team for availability and a current quotation; request the complete physical codex first, then map the work within the delivered image sequence
access.format: Digital images by in-house low-resolution photography where available or full digitization through Cambridge University Library, subject to quotation and preservation constraints
notes: Specify MS 674 and request front cover or board, spine, back cover or board, pastedowns, flyleaves, all blanks, every leaf recto and verso, inserts, stubs, detached fragments, and a color target or scale if available.
Ask Holkham to preserve or supply its authoritative foliation and image sequence so the first eleven menu leaves and the remaining calendar and recipe material can be mapped after delivery.

The currently linked 2023 schedule lists in-house low-resolution rates of GBP 18 per image for 1-2 images, GBP 12 for 3-20, and GBP 6 for 21-40; full digitization through Cambridge is listed at GBP 18 for 1-2, GBP 12 for 3-20, GBP 6 for 21-99, and GBP 3.50 for 100 or more, plus a GBP 350 transfer/administration fee, excluding VAT. Confirm all rates and the image count before ordering.
Holkham states that preservation concerns may prevent photography and that it does not maintain an in-house high-resolution photographer.
access.provider: Holkham Hall, the Earl of Leicester and the Trustees of the Holkham Estate

rights.basis: No item-specific images, delivery agreement, or reuse grant is currently recorded. Holkham's linked policy says copyright is retained, supplies images for noncommercial research or private study, and requires formal permission for reproduction or publication.
rights.component: Any new digital photographs or scans supplied by Holkham or its imaging partner in response to a reproduction order
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and the intended place of reuse
notes: Do not commit commissioned manuscript images to git unless Holkham gives written permission covering public redistribution and the repository's commercial print-on-demand context.

Preserve the written quotation, credit line, delivery terms, image creator, creation date, and permission response. If redistribution is refused, retain only the private ignored research cache and publish the access route and locators.
Public-domain status of the medieval text does not waive contractual or image-use conditions attached to supplied files.

dateStatements.label: Napier's internal-date argument
dateStatements.notes: This relative date is Napier's inference from the menus and handwriting, preserved separately from the modern museum dating.
dateStatements.value: written after the 1467 Neville feast, probably shortly afterwards

entities.description: The sole identified manuscript witness of the culinary compilation printed by Napier. It is privately held at Holkham Hall by the Earl of Leicester and the Trustees of the Holkham Estate.

holding.notes: The current Fitzwilliam exhibition label credits the Earl of Leicester and the Trustees of the Holkham Estate; no item-level Holkham online catalog record was found.

entities.label: Holkham Hall MS 674
notes: No public whole-codex or selected-leaf manuscript images were found during the date-bounded search recorded below.

A reproduction request should identify MS 674 and ask for the complete codex: front and back covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every recto and verso, and any inserted or detached material. Exact work foliation should be assigned only after those images or an authoritative foliation are available.

physicalDescription.extent: About 80 leaves according to Napier's 1881 description
notes: Napier describes a small volume written neatly in black ink, with headings and ornamentation in red.
The manuscript had been rebound by 1881 and showed damp damage in places but was otherwise in excellent condition.
The first eleven leaves contain feast menus; the remaining leaves contain household-service fragments, a calendar, and recipes.
Napier's final 1882 edition reports 27 missing recipes between 'Zoupes chett' and 'Lesk lombard'. Exact modern foliation and collation were not found in an openly accessible current catalog.

rights.basis: The manuscript is dated c. 1480-1500 and its underlying historical text is far outside any U.S. copyright term.
rights.component: The anonymous medieval textual content of the manuscript
notes: This concerns the old textual content, not modern photographs, digital files, catalog prose, transcriptions, or access agreements.

rights.basis: No item-specific images, delivery agreement, or reuse grant is currently recorded. Holkham's linked policy says copyright is retained, supplies images for noncommercial research or private study, and requires formal permission for reproduction or publication.

entities.description: The current Hall page links these 2023 PDFs for researcher access and digital-image orders. They describe enquiry procedures, preservation discretion, current linked fee schedules, and restrictions on reproduction and publication.
entities.label: Holkham Library and Archives access and image-fee policies
notes: The archive-study fee schedule should not automatically be applied to manuscript-library consultation without confirmation from Holkham.
The linked image-fee schedule is dated 2023; request a current quote before relying on it.

next_steps: Ask Holkham whether a complete existing surrogate can be supplied before commissioning new photography.
If new work is required, request the complete codex, including covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every recto and verso, stubs, inserts, and detached material.

After receipt, inventory the full image sequence and record the exact folio or image range for the feast menus, service fragments, calendar, 253 extant recipe units, final trailer, and any lost-leaf gaps.
notes: The best current descriptive boundary is approximately eighty leaves, with feast menus on the first eleven; it is not a substitute for foliation.
No public copy found is not synonymous with not digitized.
openQuestions.question: Does a complete digital or preservation facsimile of Holkham Hall MS 674 exist outside the public sources searched, and what is the manuscript's authoritative modern foliation?

next_steps: Collate Napier's complete printed transcription against a color whole-codex facsimile leaf by leaf.

Document manuscript abbreviation signs, supplied letters, punctuation, lineation, rubrication, decorative initials, corrections, and damaged or missing passages separately from Napier's representations.
Treat the title-page word 'verbatim' as Napier's edition statement until the collation is complete.
openQuestions.question: How exactly do Napier's spelling, punctuation, expansions, headings, and typography correspond to the manuscript's writing and red ornament?

next_steps: Ask expressly whether the delivered images may be placed in a public git repository that supports nonprofit and for-profit print-on-demand publication.
Retain the written response and delivery terms, and record image creator, creation date, checksum, extent, technical metadata, and required credit line.
If public redistribution is not granted, keep the images only in the ignored research cache and expose the source citation, request route, and work locators rather than the files.
openQuestions.question: What item-specific copyright, credit-line, contractual, and redistribution terms would Holkham apply to a complete MS 674 facsimile?

next_steps: Inspect surviving CME repository history and U-M bulk-release documentation for an item-level production record.
Keep the supported copied-from relation distinct from an undocumented byte-for-byte identity claim.
openQuestions.question: What exact U-M release, transfer date, and transformation history produced the pinned local CME00005 XML blob?

reviewNotes: The abstract culinary compilation, physical manuscript, 1882 edition, U-M encoding, and repository XML are separate entities with separate access and rights records.
The ignored source cache preserves whole publicly available source objects when possible; work_portion records identify the relevant pages or canvases within those whole objects.
Negative availability findings are date-bounded and do not prove that no private, preservation, or unindexed digitization exists.
Rights in medieval text, old editions, provider scans, and newly commissioned manuscript images must not be inherited from one another.

lineage.summary: The repository XML is a local copy/version of University of Michigan CME item CME00005. U-M's digital text encodes selected contents of Robina Napier's 1882 edition, whose main text transcribes the surviving culinary compilation in Holkham Hall MS 674. A complete public scan of the 1882 edition has been cached, but no public whole-manuscript facsimile of MS 674 was found as of 2026-07-11. Holkham accepts research and reproduction enquiries; a request should seek the complete codex and then map this work within it rather than ordering only presumed work leaves.

catalogSummary.author: Anonymous compiler(s)
catalogSummary.date: Compiled after 1467 according to Napier; surviving manuscript dated c. 1480-1500 by the Fitzwilliam Museum
catalogSummary.region: England; exact place of compilation and dialect region unresolved
catalogSummary.title: A Noble Boke off Cookry

catalogingSubject.label: A Noble Boke off Cookry
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The anonymous late-medieval English culinary compilation represented by CME00005. It is distinct from the physical Holkham Hall manuscript MS 674, Robina Napier's 1882 edition and its modern paratext, the University of Michigan digital encoding, and the repository XML artifact.

contentParts.label: Collection of feast menus
notes: The ten top-level menu units contain twelve nested course DIV3 elements and 337 XML ITEM elements; ITEM is a markup count, not a count of separate works.
contentParts.summary: Ten historical royal and aristocratic feast-menu units, many divided into courses, including the 1467 installation feast of George Neville as Archbishop of York.

contentParts.label: Monthly household-service fragments
contentParts.summary: January and February household services followed by a fragmentary first course.

contentParts.label: Calendar of the book of cookery
notes: The item_count is the number of XML ITEM nodes, not a canonical count of recipes. Napier reports 27 calendared recipes missing from the surviving manuscript, while the current XML encodes 253 extant recipe DIV2 units.
contentParts.summary: An alphabetical-style calendar or finding list of dishes intended to precede the recipe text.

contentParts.label: Collection of recipes
notes: The XML closes with the manuscript trailer beginning 'Her endithe a noble book of cookry'.
contentParts.summary: Two hundred fifty-three extant culinary recipe units covering pottages, sauces, meat, poultry, fish, pastries, sweets, and dishes for ordinary, feast, and fasting service.

assertion.notes: Napier dates the written compilation after the 1467 Neville feast and probably shortly thereafter; the Fitzwilliam Museum dates the surviving manuscript c. 1480-1500. This range does not date every underlying recipe.

notes: Do not normalize Napier's relative argument to a single year such as 1468.

extent.description: The central recipe collection contains 253 encoded DIV2 recipe units. The complete XML representation also contains ten top-level feast-menu DIV2 units, twelve nested course DIV3 units, three monthly-service or fragment DIV2 units, 274 calendar ITEM nodes, and 124 PB milestones. Napier states that 27 recipes represented in the calendar are missing from the surviving manuscript. These are representation and transmission counts rather than a claim that the abstract compilation originally had exactly 280 uniformly bounded recipes.

formStatements.description: Recipe directions, feast menus, service lists, and the calendar are transmitted as prose or prose lists. Brief rhyming phrases and mottos do not make the compilation mixed-form.

scope.description: French headings and mottos embedded in the feast menus

notes: The Fitzwilliam label's phrase 'over 280 recipes' is retained as its institutional description but is not used as an exact item count because Napier and the XML expose different calendar, surviving-text, and loss counts.
The medieval textual content and physical manuscript are modeled separately from Napier's editorial work and the U-M encoding.

next_steps: Consult a modern linguistic or codicological study based directly on Holkham Hall MS 674.
Keep the work's compilation context distinct from the manuscript's present Holkham location and Napier's London publication place.
openQuestions.question: Can the place of compilation and dialect region be established more narrowly than England?

next_steps: Collate the manuscript calendar, surviving recipe divisions, and any lost-leaf evidence from a complete manuscript facsimile.
Do not add the 274 XML calendar items mechanically to, or equate them with, the 253 encoded recipe units.
openQuestions.question: How many recipes did the compilation contain before the losses identified by Napier?

notes: Do not infer a place of compilation from the manuscript's present location at Holkham Hall or from the 1882 edition's London imprint.

assertion.notes: The compilation is anonymous, and its menu and recipe materials may have been assembled from more than one older source.

scope.description: The 1882 printed edition through which CME00005 transmits the compilation
Napier's main transcription of Holkham Hall MS 674 on printed pages 1-123

subjects.label: Household food service and course organization

summaries.value: An anonymous late-fifteenth-century English culinary compilation preserved in Holkham Hall MS 674. It opens with historical feast menus and household service fragments, supplies a calendar of dishes, and transmits 253 extant recipe units in the U-M encoding. Napier reports that 27 recipes once expected by the calendar are missing from the surviving manuscript.

notes: This concise established title is derived from the manuscript's closing and Napier's edition title; no separate medieval title page is known.

assertion.notes: Napier uses this wording on the 1882 edition title page; spelling and capitalization vary in modern catalogs.

titles.value: A Noble Boke off Cookry ffor a Prynce Houssolde or Eny Other Estately Houssolde

work.author: Anonymous compiler(s)
work.dateDisplay: Compiled after 1467 according to Napier; surviving manuscript dated c. 1480-1500 by the Fitzwilliam Museum

publication.embeddedTitle: A NOBLE BOKE OFF COOKRY FFOR A PRYNCE HOUSSOLDE OR ENY OTHER ESTATELY HOUSSOLDE

work.regionDisplay: England; exact place of compilation and dialect region unresolved

subjects.label: Household food service and course organization

work.summary: An anonymous late-fifteenth-century English culinary compilation preserved in Holkham Hall MS 674. It opens with historical feast menus and household service fragments, supplies a calendar of dishes, and transmits 253 extant recipe units in the U-M encoding. Napier reports that 27 recipes once expected by the calendar are missing from the surviving manuscript.
